traceCapture.notes: All final chunk records include --traces metadata. The 9fc06de5 shard uses trace records from the clean main run plus the targeted repair chunks that replaced upstream failures.

shards.notes: Main shard encountered three upstream 503/timeout failures. Clean artifact replaced those failed chunk records from a targeted repair run. Matrix cost includes the main shard rerun plus the targeted repair run; the earlier abandoned failed attempt is excluded.

scoring.notes: Agent-verified semantic matches. A finding counts when it identifies the same bug in roughly the same location as an existing corpus finding. Same-file findings about different bugs do not count. One emitted finding matched both Atlassian Connect corpus entries because it covered the shared helper bug.

scores.notes: Same plugin deprecation issue/rule enumeration without per-project access checks.
Same InternalEAFeaturesEndpoint access-control gap.
Same OAuth userinfo inactive-user/application boundary; it does not cover the hashed-token corpus entry.
System option credential logging is not the corpus allowlist tuple bug for this commit.
Same accept-invite path deleting arbitrary org members without validating the invite token.
Same client_secret_jwt expiry/replay weakness.
Same workflow-backed rule/project authorization boundary, reported on delete.
Same unsigned Bitbucket Server webhook acceptance bug.
Same Statsig webhook timestamp freshness/replay protection gap.
Bitbucket Cloud forwarded-IP spoofing is not the corpus invalid-signature HMAC logging bug.
Same Jira Server webhook JWT logging/replay exposure.
Same Atlassian Connect helper trusting the unverified JWT algorithm header.
Same legacy OAuth redirect URI prefix matching bug.
Same uuid1 release webhook token weakness.
Same Seer RPC HMAC not binding the URL/method path.
Inbound email From spoofing is not the corpus Mailgun timestamp/nonce freshness issue.
Same copy-as-curl shell injection through unescaped request metadata.
Same feature flag ECharts tooltip HTML injection.
CSV header formula injection is not the corpus tab/carriage-return sanitizer bypass.
Same ControlAccessService wrong-key SSO owner override bug.
Same Codecov repository token regeneration allowed with org:read.
Accept-invite member deletion is not the corpus shared-issue slug confusion bug for this commit.
Same ExternalTeamDetailsEndpoint org-only lookup bug.
Same MS Teams action target group/integration organization scoping bug.
Same Perforce configData password exposure.
Slack response_url logging is not the corpus Slack get_rule or starred-segment project scoping bug.
Slack team unlink authorization is not the corpus external_id identity deletion bug.
Perforce installation SSRF/probing is not the corpus Perforce password exposure bug.
Slack Autofix org-access gap is not a corpus finding for this commit.
Same preprod artifact approval missing project access.
Preprod repository/status-check forgery is not the corpus snapshot GET/DELETE project-access bug.
Same authenticated Pub/Sub-style transfer retry trigger.
Same Autofix automation settings GET bypassing accessible-project filtering.
Same merge verification code expiry/single-use gap.
Same Sentry App cell service dropping project access when user is optional.
Same Seer Autofix run_id not bound to the requested group.
Attaching inaccessible workflows is not the corpus detector disconnect permission-check bug.
Same SystemOptionsEndpoint option_value credential logging bug.

notes: Full benchmark result stitched from one Warden run per corpus SHA using --runtime pi --model openai/gpt-5.5 --effort low --traces. The 9fc06de5 shard required a targeted repair after upstream 503/timeout failures; matrix cost includes the main shard rerun plus the targeted repair run, while the earlier abandoned failed attempt is excluded. All emitted findings were manually scored by semantic match against the corpus.
